DESCRIPTION: Receive the incoming wafer lot and verify lot ID, product type, and route version.
Confirm wafer count, traveler information, and manufacturing order consistency.
Inspect wafers for visible defects, handling damage, contamination, and edge chips.
Measure incoming wafer thickness at center and edge points.
Perform particle scan to assess surface cleanliness before processing.
Remove loose particles and residues prior to wet processing.
Apply alkaline clean to remove organic contamination; monitor temperature and time.
Apply acidic clean to remove metallic residues and remaining contamination.
Remove native oxide with dilute HF and prepare a clean silicon surface.
Dry wafers using spin-rinse-dry or nitrogen drying.
Verify substrate orientation, resistivity, and starting material specification.
Prepare substrate surface for epitaxial growth with a final pre-clean.
Grow the epitaxial silicon layer; control temperature, gas flow, and deposition rate.
Measure epitaxial layer thickness and uniformity across the wafer.
Measure resistivity or sheet resistance to confirm layer properties.
Anneal the epitaxial layer to improve crystal quality and reduce defects.
Grow a thin oxide layer for masking or interface preparation.
Measure oxide thickness and uniformity by metrology.
Coat photoresist; track spin speed, thickness, and coating quality.
Remove solvent from the resist film and stabilize it for exposure.
Align the first mask for junction or window formation.
Expose the resist using the first mask; record dose and focus.
Develop exposed resist to define the first pattern.
Inspect pattern fidelity, overlay, and defect density.
Etch oxide in opened regions to create the required windows.
Remove resist after etch.
Clean residues and particles after the first etch step.
Measure critical dimension of the etched openings.
Implant p-type body region with controlled dose and energy.
Verify wafer state before thermal activation.
Drive dopants deeper into the substrate using a thermal cycle.
Activate dopants and repair implant-induced damage.
Measure junction depth and profile to confirm implant result.
Implant n-type buffer or field region for voltage support.
Confirm surface condition before the next anneal.
Activate buffer implant and stabilize the junction profile.
Measure sheet resistance of the implanted regions.
Deposit field oxide or dielectric for isolation and masking.
Densify the oxide to improve film stability and quality.
Measure dielectric thickness and uniformity.
Coat resist for the next patterning step.
Stabilize the resist film before exposure.
Align second mask for active area or contact definition.
Expose the resist to define the next pattern.
Develop resist to reveal the intended pattern.
Inspect field or active-area pattern for defects and CDs.
Etch oxide to open the defined area.
Remove resist after etch.
Clean residues and particles from the etched surface.
Measure local surface uniformity after patterning.
Implant source/contact region dopants with defined energy and dose.
Implant the opposite contact region for diode or power-switch structure.
Check wafer condition before activation anneal.
Activate implanted regions and recover crystal quality.
Verify electrical activation by sheet resistance measurement.
Deposit a dielectric layer where needed for isolation or gate structure.
Anneal dielectric to improve film properties and reduce stress.
Inspect dielectric quality, thickness, and defect density.
Deposit polysilicon gate or auxiliary conductor layer.
Anneal polysilicon to improve grain structure and film behavior.
Measure polysilicon thickness and across-wafer uniformity.
Apply photoresist for poly or conductor patterning.
Remove solvent and prepare the resist for exposure.
Align third lithography mask.
Expose resist for gate or conductor patterning.
Develop the resist to define the pattern.
Inspect the patterned feature for line integrity and CD control.
Etch polysilicon with directional dry etch.
Remove photoresist after etch.
Clean residues and prepare for dielectric formation.
Measure critical dimension and verify the target geometry.
Implant channel-stop or termination regions to improve edge behavior.
Confirm the wafer is ready for a final thermal cycle.
Activate channel-stop implant and stabilize the profile.
Measure key device parameters after implant and anneal.
Deposit interlayer dielectric for isolation and routing.
Densify dielectric to improve mechanical and electrical stability.
Measure the dielectric thickness after deposition.
Polish the dielectric to achieve planarity.
Check surface topography and remaining step height after CMP.
Coat resist for contact/via definition.
Stabilize the resist film before exposure.
Align contact/via mask to underlying features.
Expose resist to open contact locations.
Develop the resist to define via openings.
Inspect the via pattern for alignment and CD accuracy.
Etch through dielectric to open contacts.
Remove resist after via etch.
Clean residues and prepare for metallization.
Measure via critical dimension and opening quality.
Deposit barrier layer to prevent diffusion and improve adhesion.
Deposit seed layer for subsequent metal fill.
Fill vias with metal while controlling void formation.
Polish excess metal and leave planar contacts.
Measure contact resistance to confirm metallization quality.
Deposit top metal layer for interconnect or terminal formation.
Anneal metal to improve grain structure and adhesion.
Measure metal thickness and uniformity.
Coat resist for final metal patterning.
Remove solvent and stabilize resist film.
Align final metal mask.
Expose resist for terminal or routing pattern.
Develop resist to reveal final metal structure.
Inspect final metal pattern for opens, shorts, and CD variation.
Etch metal according to the designed layout.
Remove resist after metal etch.
Clean residues and particles from the metal surface.
Measure line width and spacing after etch.
Deposit passivation to protect the device from moisture and contamination.
Cure the passivation layer to stabilize its properties.
Check thickness, coverage, and defect density of the passivation.
Open bond pad windows for probing and packaging.
Pattern resist for pad openings.
Develop resist to define the pad openings.
Etch the passivation at pad sites.
Remove resist after pad opening.
Clean the pad area for electrical access.
Verify pad opening dimensions and completeness.
Thin the wafer backside to target thickness.
Measure final wafer thickness after backside thinning.
Remove grinding damage and backside residues.
Rinse the backside to remove particles and etch byproducts.
Dry the wafer thoroughly after backside wet processing.
Prepare backside for metal deposition and contact formation.
Deposit backside metal for ohmic contact and thermal performance.
Anneal backside metallization to improve adhesion and contact quality.
Measure backside contact resistance and uniformity.
Perform final clean before electrical and visual inspection.
Verify final wafer thickness and mechanical state.
Check bow, warp, and edge exclusion.
Perform final particle scan prior to test.
Run device-level electrical tests on the completed wafer.
Measure leakage current and identify failing devices.
Test breakdown voltage to verify power robustness.
Evaluate switching behavior under representative conditions.
Sort wafers into bins according to test results.
Analyze
